title: Olytic Memory Access Control
description: Canonical schema for declaring who and what can read a plugin's vault-stored data. Every plugin that writes persistent data to the vault must include a memory access declaration. The vault enforces this declaration — undeclared access defaults to private.
read_timing: runtime — read when plugin generates memory access declarations, not deploy time

plugin.description: The plugin ID whose vault data this declaration governs

access_control.description: Who can read this plugin's vault data. private = this plugin only; shared = named list of plugins; org-wide = any plugin in the org

authorized_readers.description: If access_control = 'shared', the list of plugin IDs authorized to read this plugin's vault data. Required when access_control = 'shared', ignored otherwise.

access_justification.description: Why broader-than-private access is needed. Required when access_control is 'shared' or 'org-wide'.

data_lifecycle.description: Retention policy for this plugin's vault data

retention_days.description: How many days vault entries are retained before automatic purge. Null = indefinite.

purge_on_plugin_removal.description: Whether vault data should be purged when the plugin is uninstalled

notes: Access defaults to 'private' if this declaration is absent — never assume broader access
org-wide access requires explicit business justification and security review
The vault enforces access control server-side — client-side declarations are verified against this schema
authorized_readers must list plugin IDs (kebab-case), not user IDs or org names
